```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
w1 <- sample(seq(0.15,0.35,0.05),1); w2 <- sample(seq(0.25,0.45,0.05),1)
w <- c(w1,w2,1-w1-w2); q <- sample(seq(0.02,0.12,0.01),3,replace=TRUE)
ans <- sum(w*q); vals <- round(c(ans,mean(q),sum(q),sum(w^2*q),1-ans),4)
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals); wtxt <- paste(sprintf("%.2f",w),collapse=", ")
qtxt <- paste(sprintf("%.2f",q),collapse=", ")
```

Question
========
Três máquinas produzem proporções `r wtxt` dos itens, com probabilidades de defeito `r qtxt`. Qual é a probabilidade de um item escolhido ao acaso ser defeituoso?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Pela probabilidade total,
$$P(D)=\sum_{j=1}^3P(M_j)P(D\mid M_j)
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S4Q3_probabilidade_total
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
